rulings.Case Title: FOLSOM STREET INVESTMENTS, LLC ET AL VS. MICHAEL B. ROTHENBERG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Defendant Michael B. Rothenberg
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 20, 2026, line 1. PLAINTIFFS FOLSOM STREET INVESTMENTS, LLC, CRAIG HORDLOW'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Defendant Michael B. Rothenberg. The motion by plaintiff Folsom Street Investments (FSI) for terminating sanctions against defendant Michael Rothenberg is granted. Rothenberg's answer to FSI's operative complaint is stricken and default is entered against Rothenberg as to that operative complaint. FSI will need to apply for a default judgment and prove-up any damages it seeks against Rothenberg. FSI has shown that Rothenberg has committed extensive discovery abuse in this case including violating several discovery orders and that no sanctions less than terminating sanctions will adequately address Rothenberg's discovery abuse. No opposition filed to this motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMAL KAZEMI VS. ANDREW WATT MD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 20, 2026, line 3. DEFENDANT SUTTER BAY HOSPITALS'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Defendant Sutter Bay Hospital's motion for summary judgment is granted. Sutter satisfied its summary judgment burden showing that it is entitled to judgment in its favor against plaintiff Jaml Kazemi and Kazemi has not presented any evidence creating a triable dispute. No opposition filed. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: JASON COOK VS. CF COLLECTIVE, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoena Issued To Mj Partners; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of James K. Moore
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 20, 2026, line 5. PLAINTIFF JASON COOK'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoena Issued To MJ Partners. Plaintiff' Jason Cook's motion to compel compliance with deposition subpoena issued to MJ Partners is granted. MJ Partners must produce all responsive documents in its possession, custody and control no later than February 11, 2026. MJ Partners did not file any opposition to this motion and its apparent reason for non-compliance with the subpoena lacks merit. The limited opposition filed by defendants also lacks merit since plaintiff, as a minority owner of CFC, is entitled to receive all documents requested by the subpoena. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. rulings.Case Title: CHUNLEI LIU VS. MERCEDES-BENZ FINANCIAL SERVICES USA LLC
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 20, 2026, line 12. DEFENDANT MERCEDES-BENZ FINANCIAL SERVICES USA LLC'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendant Mercedes-Benz Financial Services USA LLC's demurrer to all six causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff Chunlei Liu is sustained with leave to amend as to all six causes of action. No oppositon filed. If she can do so in good faith, Liu may file a first amended complaint no later than February 18, 2026 alleging one or more viable claims. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)
